data.title: Inari Amertron Cut to Mkt Perform From Outperform by Kenanga IB >0166.KU
Japan Investment Adviser Backs EneCoat Technologies in Private Equity Deal
data.description:
Japan Investment Adviser Co said a fund it manages has acquired shares in EneCoat Technologies, a Kyoto University spin-off developing perovskite solar cells, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The startup, founded in 2018, focuses on lightweight and flexible solar cells with high efficiency in low-light conditions, making them suitable for building walls, warehouse roofs, and other areas where traditional panels are impractical.

data.title: East Timor Inflation Rate Drops at Softer Pace
data.description:
Consumer prices in East Timor fell by 0.2% year-on-year in January 2025, marking the third consecutive month of consumer deflation but easing from a 0.4% drop in the previous month.

Prices declined at a softer pace for alcohol and tobacco (-2.1% vs 3.2% in December), furnishings, household equipment, and routine household maintenance (-0.1% vs -1.2%), and transport (-2.2% vs -2.4%).

At the same time, inflation increased for food and non-alcoholic beverages (0.2% vs 0.1%) and clothing and footwear (0.4% vs -0.3%).

On the other hand, price growth slowed significantly for recreation and culture (0.2% vs 1.9%) and education (0.4% vs 2%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices declined by 0.2% in January, reversing from a 0.2% rise in the preceding period.

data.title: Alibaba's comeback has redeeming features
data.description:
The Chinese giant's market value is up 60% to $320 bln this year. The e-commerce unit is recovering, strengthening funding for its promising cloud and AI ventures. It vindicates boss Eddie Wu's decision to shelve a breakup. But fierce competition will keep margins under pressure.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @mak_robyn on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Alibaba on February 20 reported revenue of 280 billion yuan ($38.6 billion) in the three months to end-December, an increase of 8% year-on-year. Adjusted earnings rose 6% to 51 billion yuan.

Alibaba's New York shares closed up 8% to $135.97 on February 20.

data.title: Ohsho Food Service's Nine-Month Profit Down 8%
data.description:
Ohsho Food Service reported a 7.5% year-on-year decline in profit attributable to owners of the parent to 5.21 billion yen for the nine months ended Dec 31, 2024, from 5.63 billion yen a year ago due to absences of gain on sale of non-current assets.

The food company's basic earnings per share fell to 92.16 yen from 99.74 yen a year ago, according to a delayed Tokyo Stock Exchange filing.

Net sales increased 7.8% to 81.5 billion yen from 75.5 billion yen a year earlier, marking four consecutive years of revenue growth.

For the fiscal year ending March 31, the company forecasts attributable profit to grow 0.7% to 7.97 billion yen, or 141.16 yen per share, while net sales are expected to rise 8.4% to 109.88 billion yen.

Ohsho expects to pay a year-end dividend of 25 yen per share, bringing the total annual dividend to 100 yen per share post-stock split.

data.title: Shares of HK-listed jewelry brand Laopu Gold soar to record high
data.description:
Shares of Laopu Gold rose as much as 10% on Friday to a record high, making it one of the best performers in Hong Kong, after the company posted a positive profit alert.

The company announced on Thursday that it expects to report a net profit between 1.4 billion and 1.5 billion yuan ($206.77 million) for 2024, marking a rise of 236% to 260% from 2023.

In a market where several Hong Kong consumer stock listings have failed to draw interest, Laopu Gold has emerged as a standout, increasing its value over tenfold in less than a year since its debut.

Laopu Gold, with its dual attraction as both a luxury brand and exposure to a value-preserving commodity, highlights an escalating demand for gold among wealthy Chinese consumers amidst domestic and international uncertainties.

Laopu's shares soared to a record of HK$516 ($66.38) on Friday. This compares to the public offering price of HK$40.50 in June 2024. Meanwhile, gold prices have risen over 10% this year and hit a record.

Nomura analysts said the company has the potential to become the first Chinese luxury brand.

"Laopu, leveraging its uniquely-crafted products and tailored luxurious shopping experience, has notably differentiated itself from other jewelry houses by combining pure gold and high-end jewelry designs," the bank's analysts said.

($1 = 7.2543 Chinese yuan renminbi)

($1 = 7.7737 Hong Kong dollars)

data.title: Group of mostly Asian migrants deported from US arrive in Costa Rica
data.description:
Costa Rica's government received its first group of mostly Asian migrants deported from the United States on Thursday, part of a deal with Washington to temporarily house up to 200 deportees from other nations.

The deportations are part of U.S. President Donald Trump's crackdown on unlawful migration that includes a growing number of flights to nations cooperating with him on the multinational repatriations.

The latest group of expelled migrants were flown from the city of San Diego to Costa Rican capital San Jose, from where they were sent by bus to a migrant shelter near the border with Panama.

The migrants will be allowed to stay in Costa Rica for one month, during which time officials will coordinate their voluntary return to their home countries, Deputy Security Minister Omer Badilla told reporters at the San Jose airport.

"Most of them want to return to their countries," Badilla said, adding that those who declined would have their cases addressed on an individual basis.

The group - all part of family units - were from Uzbekistan, China, Armenia, Turkey, Afghanistan, Russia, Georgia, Vietnam, Azerbaijan, Iran, Jordan, Kazakhstan and Ghana.

Up to 200 migrants from other nations deported by the U.S. are expected to be sent to Costa Rica as part of the most recent deal, President Rodrigo Chaves announced on Wednesday, citing the threat of U.S. tariffs on Costa Rican goods.

Also on Thursday U.S. authorities ordered 177 Venezuelan migrants flown from Guantanamo Bay to Honduras. They will later be sent to Venezuela.

Meanwhile, the Panamanian government said that three of the migrants it had received from the U.S. from other nations had requested asylum and that they could ultimately be received by other countries such as Canada.

data.title: Healios Plans Capital Reduction to Offset Losses
data.description:
Healios K.K approved a plan to reduce capital stock and reserves to offset carry-forward losses and lower tax liabilities, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The proposal will be presented at the March 26 shareholders' meeting.

The company plans to cut capital stock from 1.36 billion yen to 27.1 million yen and capital reserves from 1.36 billion yen to 26.1 million yen, transferring the amounts to other capital surplus.

Subsequently, 2.67 billion yen from other capital surplus will be moved to retained earnings, bringing the balance to zero.

Shares dropped over 7% in late-morning trade Friday.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2117 ET - CIMB Group's Indonesia unit could see an improved net interest margin in 1Q thanks to deposit repricing benefits from Bank Indonesia's January rate cut, Hong Leong IB analyst Chan Jit Hoong says in a note. Loan growth is expected to remain steady but unlikely to accelerate meaningfully, he reckons. With strong buffers in place, asset-quality risks appear manageable, he adds. CIMB Niaga generally contributes about 20%-25% to CIMB Group's profit before tax, he notes. CIMB's position as a large-cap, index-heavy stock makes it attractive as investors may seek refuge from uncertainties around Trump 2.0, he says. Hong Leong maintains a buy rating on CIMB and keeps the target price at MYR9.20. Shares are 0.2% lower at MYR8.33. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2059 ET - Singtel's new FY 2025 earnings guidance looks encouraging, the OCBC Investment Research team says in note. The telecom conglomerate has raised its FY 2025 EBIT growth forecast to high teens-to-low twenties from prior forecast of low double digits, the team says. Singtel is likely a key beneficiary of Singapore's drive for technology and innovation as highlighted in recent Singapore Budget, the team says. The government will allocate S$150 million for a new "Enterprise Compute Initiative," which Singtel could tap to expand its enterprise services and cloud-computing offerings. OCBC raises stock's fair value estimate to S$4.00 from S$3.85 and maintains a buy rating. Shares are 2.1% lower at S$3.30. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2046 ET - Genting Singapore's earnings growth could be supported by its new phase of expansion at Resorts World Sentosa, say Citi Research analysts in a note. RWS is expected to see significant improvement in its earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization from mid-2025 onwards, they write. There are new non-gaming amenities opening in July. The recently opened "Minion Land" is also getting very good traction and positive feedback from visitors, they add. Citi maintains the stock's buy rating and target price of S$1.14. Shares are at S$0.76.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

2042 ET - Petronas Dagangan may face risks to long-term demand from a potential expansion of Malaysia's RON95 fuel subsidy reforms, as well as from decarbonization efforts and the electric-vehicle transition trend, TA Securities analyst Hafriz Hezry says in a note. The impending subsidy cut on its own is expected to affect only the top 15% of earners in Malaysia, with likely minimal immediate impact on the Malaysian downstream petroleum product retailer's sales volume as these consumers are unlikely to significantly reduce consumption, he says. TA Securities downgrades Petronas Dagangan to sell from hold following share-price gains over the past three months. It maintains a MYR18.20 target price. Shares are 5.5% higher at MYR20.32. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2041 ET - Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rises 1.8% to 22976.08 in early trade, led by tech stocks. Market sentiment is likely boosted by Alibaba's earnings which showed strong quarterly profits driven by growth in its cloud unit and e-commerce, the Saxo APAC research team writes in a note. Among advancers, Alibaba Group shares rise 8.7%, Tencent gains 3.3% and JD.com is 3.0% higher. The Hang Seng Tech Index adds 2.7%. Meanwhile, Henderson Land Development falls 1.9%, Sands China loses 1.7% and CK Asset sheds 1.3%. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

2041 ET - Chinese shares are higher in early trade amid positive sentiment boosted by Alibaba's earnings. Investors are positive about long-term prospects of Chinese tech stocks for their applications with artificial intelligence. Semiconductor and tech stocks are leading the gains with Cambricon Technologies up 4.3%, Huaqin Technology rising 4.9% and Hundsun Technologies adding 2.7%. Chinese auto stocks and home appliance stocks are leading the losses with Seres Group and SAIC Motor each down 1.2%. Haier Smart Home is off 0.3%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index rises 0.1% to 3355.20, the Shenzhen Composite Index gains 0.4% and the ChiNext Price Index is up 0.7%. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

2028 ET - Beacon Lighting is nearing an inflection point in the rate of earnings growth, says Morgans, as momentum in its trade business continues and lower interest rates give retail sales a shot in the arm. Analyst Emily Porter says Beacon Lighting has around 5% of a A$2 billion trade market for lighting and ceiling fans in Australia. "Building up share from this low base can more than offset the effect of a subdued overall market," says Morgans, which has an add call on the stock. The bank also points to signs that the retail cycle is beginning to turn. "We expect this to gather force as the recent interest rate cut by the Reserve Bank of Australia stimulates a recovery in consumer confidence," Morgans says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2017 ET - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index edges 0.1% lower to 3923.06, tracking Wall Street's losses overnight. Disappointing sales guidance for the coming year from bellwether U.S. retail has raised worries over the U.S. consumption outlook, Commerzbank Research analysts say in a research report. Among the worst performers on the benchmark index, Singtel falls 2.7% and Seatrium drops 2.4%.Wilmar International loses 1.9% after reporting significantly lower net profit in 2024 than the prior year. Meanwhile, Singapore Airlines rises 2.2% after posting record revenue in fiscal 3Q. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2015 ET - Chalice Mining's metallurgical breakthrough signals lower operating costs at its Gonneville deposit in Western Australia and reduced complexity, but it may not satisfy investors, suggests Barrenjoey. Chalice says it's now able to produce copper and nickel concentrates from low-grade composites, removing the need for a high-cost hydrometallurgical process. Analyst Richard Knights says Gonneville could now generate a 19% internal rate of return. "However, our project net present value is still comfortably less than Chalice's market cap (A$565 million), even after modelling a debt funded doubling of capacity after four years of operation." Chalice needs a step-change in palladium prices or an ability to feed higher grade material to the plant for longer to excite investors, Barrenjoey adds. It retains an underweight call on the stock. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2012 ET - Inari Amertron could face further margin compression in 2H FY2025 due to rising overhead costs and a ramp-up in new product introductions, Maybank IB analysts Anand Pathmakanthan and Arvind Jayaratnam say in a note. They highlight that Inari may struggle to achieve its high single-digit segmental growth target with its anchor radio frequency segment posting its lowest 1H revenue in four years. The analysts cut Inari's FY2025-FY2027 earnings forecast by 26%, 14%, and 8%, respectively, citing a softer outlook. Maybank lowers Inari's target price to MYR2.70 from MYR2.93 but maintains a hold rating, noting that downside risk could be limited after a 16% share price decline since December last year. Shares are 3.8% lower at MYR2.55. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2007 ET - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index edges 0.1% higher to 1578.86. The KLCI may stay under pressure after Wall Street's overnight weakness and amid ongoing foreign fund outflows and weak sentiment, Apex Securities says in a note. However, this trend may be temporary as Malaysian stocks remain attractively valued, which could spur bargain hunting, the brokerage says. Investors should consider defensive sectors like REITs and financials for stable dividend yields and resilience against volatility, it adds. Among gainers, YTL Corp. adds 3.0% and Petronas Dagangan is up 2.2%. Meanwhile, Press Metal Aluminium is 0.8% lower and 99 Speed Mart Retail is down 2.1%. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1945 ET - Sonic Healthcare's earnings guidance looks achievable, if not conservative, to Morgans after the pathology services company took another step toward turning a corner in the pandemic's aftermath. Sonic expects FY 2025 Ebitda of A$1.70 billion-A$1.75 billion when currency swings are stripped out, and Morgans believes that strong underlying revenue growth supports that outlook. "After years of trying to right-size the cost base to better reflect the post Covid-19 world, labor costs are 'just about there', with operating leverage returning and profitability improving," analyst Derek Jellinek says. The bank is also upbeat about the potential for acquisitions and contract gains through the medium term. Sonic is down 1.4%. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: CIMB's Indonesia Unit Could Grow NIM in 1Q as Rates Fall — Market Talk
data.description: CIMB Group's Indonesia unit could see an improved net interest margin in 1Q thanks to deposit repricing benefits from Bank Indonesia's January rate cut, Hong Leong IB analyst Chan Jit Hoong says in a note. Loan growth is expected to remain steady but unlikely to accelerate meaningfully, he reckons. With strong buffers in place, asset-quality risks appear manageable, he adds. CIMB Niaga generally contributes about 20%-25% to CIMB Group's profit before tax, he notes. CIMB's position as a large-cap, index-heavy stock makes it attractive as investors may seek refuge from uncertainties around Trump 2.0, he says. Hong Leong maintains a buy rating on CIMB and keeps the target price at MYR9.20. Shares are 0.2% lower at MYR8.33. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Vision to Offer Wi-Fi and Mobile Charging Stores at Expo 2025 Osaka
data.description:
Vision will put up tores at Expo 2025 Osaka, Kansai, Japan, offering rental Wi-Fi and mobile battery services, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The company will operate in the East and West Gate Zones, supplying automated, cashless rental and vending machines for its GLOBAL WiFi and NINJA WiFi services.

data.title: Deep Yellow Says Rights of Unit's Mining Licence to be Challenged in High Court of Namibia; Shares Rise 5%
data.description:
Deep Yellow announced that individual Jurgen Hoffman and his associated entity Tumas Granite intend to challenge the rights to the Company's mining licence (ML237), and its environmental clearance certificate held by its unit, in Namibia's High Court, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The applicants seek to review, correct, or annul the Environmental Commissioner's decision to award the environmental certificate for ML237, and the Minister of Mines and Energy's decision to grant ML237 to the company, the filing said.

Hoffman and Tumas Granite also seek orders from the Court to declare these decisions unconstitutional, rendering them null and void.

The company maintains that the challenge is without merit and will take necessary action to protect its interests.

Shares of the company rose 5% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Australia's Latitude Group gains on FY profit and dividend
data.description:
** Shares of Latitude Group up 3.5% at A$1.19

** Stock rises as much as 8.7% earlier in the day, hitting its highest level since June 13, 2023

** Stock posts its biggest intraday gain since January 30, 2023

** Lending business says it achieved FY24 statutory NPAT from continuing operations of A$30.6 million ($19.56 million), compared to a loss of A$102.7 million in FY23

** Co declares a final dividend of 3 Australian cents per share; dividend was paused in FY23

** LFS up 3.5% YTD

($1 = 1.5642 Australian dollars)

data.title: COMMENT-USD/JPY through 150.00 as JGB yields march higher
data.description:
The prognosis for USD/JPY is a lower range going forward after it broke below 150.00 Thursday for the first time since December 2024. Retracements are to be expected but the ceiling looks to be lower now with many eyeing 152.00.

Expectations of more Bank of Japan policy rate hikes are getting stronger each day, and Japanese government bond yields have been marching higher as a result. JGB-U.S. Treasury interest rate differentials have narrowed considerably, and could tighten more with the U.S. Federal Reserve in pause mode and U.S. yields mostly sideways while JGB yields continue firming.

The market still sees the chance of a BOJ hike in March as slim but expectations for a move in May, June or July have risen considerably. An early hike could also prompt expectations of more this year, depending on economic and inflation data, of course.

USD/JPY moving relatively easily through 150.00 Thursday and to 149.29 EBS in Asia Friday surprised many in the market. Many Tokyo players were expecting heavy bids on the 150 handle to curtail a break below, especially with the market recently having gone long yen.

USD/JPY has since recovered, bouncing to 150.45 on short-covering ahead of Tokyo's long weekend. More retracements up cannot be ruled out but the ceiling looks to be lower now with considerable resistance eyed above 152.00, the site of recent peaks.

The overall USD/JPY bias looks to remain down however with specs already eyeing an eventual break below 149.00 to 148.65, the spike low on December 3, 2024. The weekly Ichimoku cloud between 151.11-78 next week looks to work as pivot. Previous comments , , , .

  • For more click on

data.title: Hong Kong Shares Point to 6th Straight Weekly Gains
data.description:
Hong Kong's stocks jumped 542 points or 2.4% to 23,115 in early deals on Friday, reversing losses in the prior session amid robust gains in most sectors notably consumers and tech.

The Hang Seng is on track to post its sixth consecutive week of gains, up around 2% so far, marking the longest winning streak in almost a year.

Enthusiasm over China's AI sector, particularly following the emergence of DeepSeek, continues to boost sentiment.

data.title: Japan futures set for weekly gains on tighter supply outlook
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures edged up on Friday and were set for modest weekly gains amid prospects of weaker supply from top producer Thailand, though a firmer yen kept a lid on prices.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for July delivery , was up 2 yen, or 0.53%, at 377 yen ($2.51) per kg, as of 0205 GMT. The contract has gained 0.11% so far this week.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for May delivery rose 185 yuan, or 1.03%, to 18,070 yuan ($2,491.07) per metric ton.

  • The most active February butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) nudged down 85 yuan, or 0.61%, to 13,865 yuan ($1,911.39) per metric ton.

  • Top rubber producer Thailand's meteorological agency issued a caution for farmers to prepare for crop damage, and the South should beware of heavy rains and accumulations from February 22-26.

  • From a seasonal perspective, overseas production areas have gradually stopped harvesting and raw material prices are high, supporting prices, Chinese financial data provider Tonghuashun Information said in a note.

  • Rubber crops usually undergo a season of low production from February to May, before a peak harvesting period that lasts until September.

  • In top consumer China, the country will direct more efforts to boosting consumption, Premier Li Qiang said on Thursday, vowing to adopt more targeted measures to stimulate consumer spending.

  • U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday a trade deal with China is possible, adding that he expected Chinese President Xi Jinping to visit the United States.

  • Meanwhile, the yen broke through chart resistance at 150 per dollar overnight, strengthening as far as 149.285 per dollar.

  • A stronger currency makes yen-denominated assets less affordable to overseas buyers.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for March delivery last traded at 208.8 U.S. cents per kg, up 1.3%.

($1 = 150.2700 yen)

($1 = 7.2539 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Solaris Energy Infra 2024 Rev $313.1M >SEI
Industrial & Infrastructure Fund Completes Hamura Logistics Center Redevelopment
data.description:
Industrial & Infrastructure Fund Investment completed the redevelopment of its Hamura Logistics Center in Tokyo, acquiring the upgraded facility for 3.15 billion yen, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The four-story warehouse, built by Suzuyo Construction, spans 12,895 square meters and is fully leased to Tokyo Logistics Factory Co. under a 10-year contract.

The company said the project improves asset value by utilizing the surplus floor-area ratio while improving energy performance.

data.title: WA1 Resources Discovers High-Grade Niobium Mineralization at Western Australia Project
data.description:
WA1 Resources discovered a high-grade niobium zone east of the Luni deposit in its West Arunta project in Western Australia through a resource definition drill program, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Assay results include 16.9 meters at 3.7% niobium from 43.4 meters, 50.2 meters at 2.8% niobium from 51 meters, and 16 meters at 3% niobium from 39 meters, the filing said.

Diamond drilling is expected to resume at the project soon, with two additional drilling rigs planned to be mobilized, according to the filing.

Shares rose nearly 2% in midday trade Friday.

data.title: Alibaba's Hong Kong shares hit over 3-year high after revenue beat
data.description:
** Hong Kong shares of Alibaba Group jump as much as 11.2% to HK$134.50, their highest since November 2021 and on course to snap two sessions of declines

** Stock on track for the biggest one-day pct gain since March 2023; top pct gainer in the Hang Seng Index , Hang Seng Tech Index and Hang Seng China Enterprises Index , which rise 1.8%, 2.5% and 1.9%, respectively

** Co's U.S.-listed stock rose 8.1% to end at $135.97 on Thursday, the highest close since January 2022

** Alibaba on Thursday flagged plans to invest more in its "core businesses" of e-commerce and AI-powered cloud computing after reporting third-quarter revenues just above analysts' estimates on strong year-end sales

** Citi maintains "buy" rating and lifts TP on Alibaba's U.S shares to $170 from $138 and the Hong Kong stock to HK$166 from HK$137

** Alibaba is well-positioned to capture the influx demand of cloud computing service in the coming years, Citi said in a note, adding there remains upside risks to the pace of cloud revs acceleration and profitability improvement

** Hong Kong-listed stock up 60% YTD, U.S. shares up 60.4%

data.title: Sumco Sets Year-end Dividend at 6 Yen per Share
data.description:
Sumco approved a year-end dividend of 6 yen per share for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024, in line with its prior forecast, according to a Thursday filing with the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The total annual payout, including the 15-yen interim dividend, amounts to 21 yen per share, down from 55 yen a year earlier.

The company cited profit levels, future outlook, capital investment needs, and internal reserves as factors in its decision. The dividend will be paid on March 7.

data.title: One REIT Completes First Transfer of MY Kumamoto Building Stake
data.description:
One REIT completed the first tranche of the proposed transfer of a 45% quasi-co-ownership interest in MY Kumamoto Building for 841 million yen, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The transaction is part of a two-step sale plan.

data.title: Stocks in Hong Kong Hit 19-week High
data.description:
HK50 increased to a 19-week high of 23143.00 Index Points.

Over the past 4 weeks, Hong Kong Stock Market Index (HK50) gained 16.73%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 37.9%.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 9 PM ET
data.description:
Dow, S&P 500 Move Lower as Walmart Stock Falls

Major stock indexes fell, with investors unsettled by a note of caution in Walmart's forecast. Big bank stocks fell sharply despite no clear news catalyst, which some analysts attributed to economic concerns..

Musk Looks at Auditing the Federal Reserve

The billionaire gives remarks at the Conservative Political Action Conference.

Uptick in Japan Inflation Boosts Rate Hike Hopes

Japan's consumer inflation increased in January because of higher energy and food costs, likely adding fuel to speculation that an interest-rate hike is coming soon.

Australia Central Bank Says it Won't Commit to Any Set Course on Rates

The Reserve Bank of Australia isn't committed to any course of action on interest rates with Gov. Michele Bullock cautioning that easing policy too quickly may stoke a fresh wave of inflationary pressures.

U.K. Consumers Feel Small Boost From BOE Rate Cut, Survey Suggests

U.K. consumers were slightly less gloomy this month about expectations for their personal finances, although the outlook for the economy continued to be a source of pessimism.

U.S. Aluminum Buyers Scramble for Metal as Trump Tariff Looms

Canada supplies roughly 60% to 70% of the imports that come into the U.S. market. Canadian suppliers like Rio Tinto produce high quality aluminum that is typically sold to stateside buyers.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories increased by more than expected last week in a fourth consecutive build, while product stocks declined, according to U.S. government data.

Funding Cuts at CFPB Seen Leading to 'Regulatory Vacuum' for Big Banks

Turmoil at the agency could harm not only consumers but also small state-chartered banks, as their bigger rivals gain advantage from lax CFPB supervision, 23 state attorneys general say in court filing.

Bank of Mexico Minutes Suggest More Half-Point Rate Cuts Likely

The central bank cut its benchmark rate to 9.5% from 10% on Feb. 6 as board members noted the economy's contraction in the fourth quarter and the wild card of U.S. policy.

There are growing signs that the labor market is slowing, Fed's Bostic says

There are "potential vulnerabilities" that could be worrying going forward.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 9 PM ET
data.description:
Celsius to Buy Rival Energy-Drink Maker Alani Nu for $1.8 Billion

The deal adds a fast-growing brand popular with young women to Celsius's product line as its own growth slows.

Ryan Cohen Boosts Alibaba Stake to $1 Billion

Ryan Cohen still has his sights set on Alibaba Group and has been building up his position in the Chinese e-commerce behemoth.

Rivian Unveils Gross Profit, Weak Guidance. The Stock Is Up.

Sales came in at $1.7 billion, while Wall Street was looking for $1.5 billion.

FTC Opens Inquiry Into Censorship at Tech Companies

The FTC issued a request for information from consumers, including those who have been banned from platforms.

Canadian Trucker TFI Plans Move to the U.S.

TFI International plans to move its legal registration to the U.S. from Canada, a potential blow for the Canadian economy as companies consider whether to move operations southward amid President Trump's tariff threats.

What Is Quantum Computing, and Why Does It Matter?

Microsoft and Google have made breakthroughs in quantum computing, a technology that could revolutionize drug discovery and other fields.

Amazon MGM Studios Gains Creative Control of James Bond Franchise From Broccoli Family

Amazon will now control who will play 007, who will write the next script, and when the film goes into production-three critical pieces that had been held up by a yearslong stalemate.

Leonardo Eyes Joint Ventures to Meet Boost in Europe's Defense Orders

Leonardo is preparing to ramp up production through new joint ventures and industrial alliances ahead of an expected spike in European orders for military equipment.

Hasbro Expects Mild Sales Growth in 2025 as Tariffs Loom

Hasbro is guiding for revenue to tick up slightly in 2025 as it navigates a looming trade war and adopts a new strategy to spur sales.

Walmart Warns of Slower Sales Gains After a Bumper Year

The company's shares retreated after executives set 2026 fiscal-year revenue and profit targets below analysts' expectations.

data.title: Lost Money on Voyager Therapeutics, Inc. (VYGR)? You May Have Been Affected by Fraud - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Voyager Therapeutics, Inc. ("Voyager Therapeutics, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

Voyager issued a press release on February 11, 2025, "announc[ing] it has decided to assess alternate payloads related to its gene therapy program for superoxide dismutase 1 (SOD1) amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS)", stating that "[e]merging preclinical data indicate the siRNA payload component of VY9323 does not meet our high standards due to what appears to be an off-target effect resulting in a narrowed therapeutic window." Voyager further stated that it "no longer anticipates filing an investigational new drug (IND) application for VY9323 in mid-2025."

Following this new, Voyager's stock price fell over 20% on February 11, 2025. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/voyager-therapeutics-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131110&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. either via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or by telephone at (212)363-7500.

data.title: NZX on track for highest one-day gain in 3 years on upbeat FY profit
data.description:
** Shares of bourse operator NZX rise 4.4% to NZ$1.65, highest level since November 18

** Stock set for biggest one-day pct gain since late-February 2022

** Co posts total net profit of NZ$25.5 million ($14.69 million) for FY24, compared to NZ$13.6 million a year earlier

** Says NZX saw pick-up in capital market activity in 2H24 as interest rates began to fall

** Forecasts FY25 operating earnings of NZ$49 million-NZ$54 million

** More than 262,000 shares change hands vs 30-day avg volume of 182,000 shares

** NZX up 13% YTD, including current session's moves

($1 = 1.7355 New Zealand dollars)

NOTES: The State Bank of Vietnam sets the mid-point rate on a daily basis and allows dollar/dong transactions to move in a band of +/- 5% around the mid point.

The dong's exchange rate against other currencies is not restricted by a band. Interbank offered rates are the latest indicative bid/ask prices, quoted from market sources.

One tael is equivalent to 37.5 grams or 1.21 troy ounces. SJC gold prices are quoted by state-owned Saigon Jewelry Co.

For more interbank rate fixings released at 0400 GMT, click on (VNIBOR-1).

data.title: Solaris Energy Infra 4Q EPS 19c >SEI
Mayne Pharma board backs $672 million takeover bid from Cosette Pharma
data.description: Mayne Pharma has received a takeover offer at a price of $7.40 per share from US-based pharmaceutical company Cosette Pharma, valuing the company at $672 million. Cosette has a portfolio of products in women’s health and dermatology and a long history of manufacturing complex dosage forms including topical creams, ointments, oral liquids/solutions and suppositories. The US-based firm is backed by healthcare-focused private equity firm Avista Healthcare Partners and funds controlled by private markets investment management firm Hamilton Lane. Excellent work reflected Mayne Pharma chief executive officer Shawn Patrick O’Brien said this was a pivotal moment in Mayne’s journey to improve patient access to life-enhancing medications. “Attracting an offer from a strategic buyer who is active in the US dermatology and women’s health markets, such as Cosette, reflects the excellent work our teams have been doing to strengthen our company over the last year and more,” chair Frank Condella added. Cosette has corporate and manufacturing facilities in New Jersey and North Carolina and is supported by more than 350 team members across all operations. Board recommendation The Mayne Pharma board has unanimously recommended that shareholders vote in favour of the scheme, which is fully funded and subject to limited customary conditions. “The board believes that the proposed transaction is in line with its priority to deliver value to our shareholders and also provides significant benefits for our broader stakeholders,” Mr Condella said. The $7.40 offer price represents a premium of 37% to Mayne Pharma’s closing share price as at 20 February 2025 and a 42% premium to the 30-day volume weighted average price. Expanded global reach Cosette Pharma chief executive officer Apurva Saraf said the acquisition marked a transformational step for the company, adding patented, high-growth products to solidify its leadership in women’s health in the US while expanding its reach globally. “By combining Cosette’s strong portfolio with Mayne Pharma’s proven commercial expertise, the combined company will be well-positioned to further invest in innovation, portfolio expansion and better serve our patients,” he said. “We look forward to a timely closing and welcoming Mayne Pharma to the Cosette family.”
